key_insight: The paper shows that agentic theorem-proving workflows can formalize Shor’s algorithm family in Lean and derive machine-checked resource estimates for attacking RSA-2048 and P-256.
engineering_relevance: It demonstrates a path toward trustworthy AI-assisted quantum cryptanalysis and reusable formal verification pipelines for complex quantum algorithms and cryptographic security analyses.
method: Agents search sources, generate Lean code, and repair proofs with human review of claims.
Formalize quantum order-finding, modular arithmetic, and elliptic-curve arithmetic in Lean.
Prove logical resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256 based on prior literature.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a machine-checked Lean formalization spanning Shor-style algorithms and resource estimates grounded in Quantum 5 and ASIACRYPT 2017 results.
watch_item: The results depend on a human-in-the-loop workflow and on the accuracy of the underlying scientific assumptions and resource models, so implementation overhead and model transferability remain concerns.
paper_title: Building Shor's Algorithm in Lean: An Agentic Formalization of Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P-256
